key_insight: The paper gives the first finite-time convergence guarantee for the original Adam optimizer in nonsmooth nonconvex settings, including the bias-correction step and heavy-tailed noise.
engineering_relevance: This strengthens the theoretical justification for using standard Adam in practical machine learning problems where objectives are nonsmooth and noisy.
method: Uses an Online-to-Nonconvex Conversion framework to analyze Adam beyond smooth objectives.
Keeps the classical Adam update intact, including bias correction and no extra clipping or modification.
Employs a randomly scaled learning rate to prove convergence under the setting β1 = β2.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a provable convergence rate of 1/T^{2/13} for nonsmooth nonconvex optimization, with guarantees extending to heavy-tailed noise.
watch_item: The guarantee is derived under the special parameter choice β1 = β2, so the result may not directly cover all common Adam hyperparameter settings.
paper_title: Adam Converges in Nonsmooth Nonconvex Optimization
